description_long: This axe features a robust wooden handle with a natural, slightly irregular texture, suggesting it is hand-carved or minimally processed. The head of the axe is constructed from a dark, roughly hewn stone, securely fastened to the handle with what appears to be thick, fibrous bindings, possibly made from plant material or leather. The blade is asymmetrical, with a broad, blunt edge on one side and a more tapered, pointed end on the other, giving it a primitive, utilitarian appearance. Moss or lichen-like textures are visible on the bindings and parts of the handle, adding to its weathered, rustic look. The overall design is reminiscent of early stone tools, with a focus on function over form, and no visible modern manufacturing marks. This axe would likely be found in settings such as urban parks, themed playgrounds, or as a decorative or prop item in garages or workshops.
description: A primitive-style axe with a wooden handle, stone blade, and fibrous bindings, featuring a weathered, rustic appearance with mossy textures.
description_view_0: Front view shows the stone blade centered and tightly bound to the handle, with visible mossy textures and the handle's flat face.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the thickness of the stone blade and the layered bindings, with the handle's curvature more apparent.
description_view_2: Angled view reveals the asymmetry of the blade and the overlapping bindings, as well as the irregular surface of the handle.
description_view_3: Rear view displays the back of the stone blade and the full width of the bindings, with the handle's grain and texture visible.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical wooden handle with a rough, natural finish and slight curvature; no added grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single, asymmetrical stone blade with a broad, blunt edge and a pointed end; no beard or poll; primitive, functional design.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional, with the blade lashed to the handle using thick plant fiber bindings.
